author: y4rr
content:
Spun out of #596. That issue landed standalone \`IntTensor<I>\` (i32 / i64) and \`BoolTensor\` types in \`ferrotorch-core\` with construction, casting, reshape, conversion, and pointwise logical ops. The follow-up is to thread these new types through the existing op surface so callers don't have to manually cast.

## Targets

### Indexing-style ops
- \`index_select_1d\` / \`index_select\` — currently take \`&[usize]\`; should accept \`&IntTensor<i64>\`
- \`gather(input, dim, index)\` — currently takes \`Tensor<T>\` for index, with a manual \`as i64\` cast inside; should take \`&IntTensor<i64>\` and skip the cast
- \`scatter\` / \`scatter_add\` — same
- \`embedding(weight, input)\` — input is naturally an \`IntTensor<i64>\` of token ids
- \`one_hot(indices, num_classes)\` — already in \`ferrotorch-nn::functional\`; switch to \`IntTensor\` input

### Mask-style ops
- \`masked_fill(t, mask, value)\` — \`mask\` should be \`&BoolTensor\` instead of \`Tensor<T>\` with implicit \`x != 0\` semantics
- \`where(cond, a, b)\` — \`cond\` → \`&BoolTensor\`
- \`masked_select(t, mask)\` — \`mask\` → \`&BoolTensor\`

### Comparison ops that produce masks
- \`gt(a, b)\`, \`lt\`, \`eq\`, \`ne\`, \`ge\`, \`le\` — currently return \`Tensor<T>\` with 0.0 / 1.0; should return \`BoolTensor\`. Add a \`Tensor::*_bool\` variant or change the return type wholesale.

## Migration strategy
Each op gets a new \`*_int\` / \`*_bool\` variant (taking the new types) alongside the existing variant. Internally, the new variant is the canonical impl; the old one calls into it with an inline cast for backward compatibility. Once all callers migrate, the legacy variants can be deprecated.

## Why split out from #596
The standalone types are useful on their own (callers can build masks, hold indices, call \`count_true\`, etc.) and don't introduce any breaking change. The op-level rewiring touches dozens of call sites and benefits from being a focused PR.
